title: Callicarpa lamii
taxonomy: Callicarpa lamii is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This family is commonly known as the mint family due to the characteristic fragrance and appearance of its members. The Lamiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, containing over 7,000 species. Callicarpa lamii itself is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. Its classification also includes the subfamily Lamioideae and the order Lamiales.
uses: Callicarpa lamii, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its leaves and stems are reportedly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its potential health benefits. Further studies are necessary to establish the plant's safety and efficacy for human use.

intro: Callicarpa lamii, also known as Purple Fruit Callicarpa, is a species of flowering plant in the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds. Bioactive constituents found in Callicarpa lamii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Callicarpa lamii is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into poultices and topical applications for skin and wound care.
